title: anthemis scrobicularis subsp. scrobicularis
intro: Anthemis scrobicularis subsp. scrobicularis, also known as the Corn Marigold or Cornflower, is a plant species in the daisy family. It is a flowering plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Anthemis scrobicularis subsp. scrobicularis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
